title: Prenzlauer Berg - Quick Summary
items: **Prenzlauer Berg is Berlin's café-and-market district in the former East**, just north of the centre and about ten minutes from Alexanderplatz on the U2. Come for the streets, markets and Sunday feeling, not one single big sight.
**Enter at Eberswalder Straße (U2).** Under the yellow elevated line is Konnopke's Imbiss, a currywurst stand run by the same family since 1930. It is open Monday to Saturday and closed on Sundays.

**The Kulturbrauerei is a free stop.** The old brick brewery holds the Museum in der Kulturbrauerei on everyday life in East Germany: free entry, Tuesday to Sunday, and one of the best-value stops in the district.

**Kollwitzplatz has a weekly market on Thursdays and Saturdays.** More than fifty stalls of Brandenburg produce and cooked food, with benches to eat at. Buy lunch from a stall rather than a restaurant.

**Two quiet landmarks sit nearby.** The Wasserturm (1877) is Berlin's oldest water tower on a small park, with a dark early-Nazi history; Prater Garten at Kastanienallee 7 is Berlin's oldest beer garden, pouring since 1837.
**Save Mauerpark for a Sunday.** The former Wall death strip hosts a big flea market and the open-air Bearpit Karaoke every Sunday afternoon.
